card.intuition: A testing technique that takes a screenshot of your UI and compares it pixel-by-pixel with a 'baseline' (the known good version) to detect accidental visual changes.
card.analogy: 'Spot the Difference' puzzles: you have two almost-identical pictures. A visual regression tool is a computer that circles every single pixel that changed between the old version and the new version instantly.
when_to_use: Preventing layout shifts during large CSS refactors
Ensuring UI consistency across different browsers and operating systems
Catching bugs that don't cause code errors but look 'broken' to the user
gotchas: Dynamic data (like the current time or random user IDs) will cause tests to fail every time. You must 'mask' these areas in your screenshots
Small rendering differences in fonts between macOS and Windows can cause 'False Positives'

exercise.prompt: Identify the popular Playwright tool used for visual comparisons.
